nodes.name: 皮乌拉大区
nodes.summary: 皮乌拉大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联15种鸟类，包括淡眉穴䳍、Ecuadorian piculet、Red-faced parrot等15种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 马纳比省
nodes.summary: 马纳比省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联9种鸟类，包括淡眉穴䳍、Ecuadorian piculet、Velvety manakin等9种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Ecuadorian piculet
nodes.summary: Ecuadorian piculet（Ecuadorian piculet，学名 Picumnus sclateri），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括灌丛，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: 兰巴耶克大区
nodes.summary: 兰巴耶克大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联8种鸟类，包括秘鲁蜂鸟、Ecuadorian piculet、Laughing falcon等8种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 瓜亚斯省
nodes.summary: 瓜亚斯省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联8种鸟类，包括淡眉穴䳍、Ecuadorian piculet、Cinnamon woodpecker等8种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in western Ecuador from Manabí Province to northern Guayas Province

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in western Ecuador from Manabí Province to northern Guayas Province

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: porcullae is found in northwestern Peru's departments of Piura and Lambayeque. The Ecuadorian piculet primarily inhabits deciduous forest and arid scrublands, though in Loja it also occurs in more humid landscapes

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: porcullae is found in northwestern Peru's departments of Piura and Lambayeque. The Ecuadorian piculet primarily inhabits deciduous forest and arid scrublands, though in Loja it also occurs in more humid landscapes

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: porcullae is found in northwestern Peru's departments of Piura and Lambayeque. The Ecuadorian piculet primarily inhabits deciduous forest and arid scrublands, though in Loja it also occurs in more humid landscapes

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: No immediate threats have been identified. It is described as uncommon in both countries and "[h]uman activity has little short-term direct effect on [the] Ecuadorian Piculet, other than the local effects of habitat destruction."
